subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Area Between Curves, Volume of Solid & Related Rates of Cross-Section
stimulus: Let $f$ and $g$ be the functions defined by $f(x) = \ln(x+3)$ and $g(x) = x^4 + 2x^3$. The graphs of $f$ and $g$ intersect at $x = -2$ and $x = B$, where $B > 0$.

parts.prompt: Find the area of the region enclosed by the graphs of $f$ and $g$.
parts.scoring_notes: Second point requires lower limit $-2$ and upper limit as letter $B$ or a value accurate to at least one decimal between $0$ and $1$. If $g(x) - f(x)$ used, must resolve the negative sign to earn third point. Must earn first point to be eligible for third.

scoring_points.description: Correct limits: lower limit $-2$, upper limit $B \approx 0.782$

required_elements: Correct limits: lower limit $-2$, upper limit $B \approx 0.782$

parts.prompt: For $-2 \le x \le B$, let $h(x)$ be the vertical distance between the graphs of $f$ and $g$. Is $h$ increasing or decreasing at $x = -0.5$? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: If a value of $h'(-0.5)$ is given, it must be correct for digits reported. The last line alone ('$h'(-0.5) < 0$ so decreasing') earns both points. Presenting only $h'(x)$ without evaluation does not earn either point.

scoring_points.description: Considers $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$ or computes $h'(-0.5) = f'(-0.5) - g'(-0.5) \approx -0.600$

required_elements: Considers $h'(x) = f'(x) - g'(x)$ or computes $h'(-0.5) = f'(-0.5) - g'(-0.5) \approx -0.600$

parts.prompt: The region enclosed by the graphs of $f$ and $g$ is the base of a solid. Cross sections of the solid taken perpendicular to the $x$-axis are squares. Find the volume of the solid.
parts.scoring_notes: First point earned for integrand $k(f(x)-g(x))^2$ with $k \ne 0$; if $k \ne 1$, ineligible for second point. Presentation error (mismatched parentheses, misplaced exponent) does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer.

wrong_examples: does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer
does not earn first point but earns second for correct answer

parts.prompt: A vertical line in the $xy$-plane travels from left to right along the base of the solid described in part (c). The vertical line is moving at a constant rate of $7$ units per second. Find the rate of change of the area of the cross section above the vertical line with respect to time when the vertical line is at position $x = -0.5$.
parts.scoring_notes: Cross-section area $A(x) = (f(x)-g(x))^2$. $A'(x) = 2(f(x)-g(x))(f'(x)-g'(x))$. First point earned for presenting $\frac{dA}{dx}\cdot\frac{dx}{dt}$, $A'(x)\cdot 7$, or equivalent. Second point: correct answer $-9.272$ or $-9.271$.
